rulings.Case Title: CELINA PHAM et al VS. JANET REMOLONA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Tamera Carlin And Jonathan Fornaci Or Int Eh Alt/Protective Order Against Further Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 28, 2011, line 1, PLAINTIFFS CELINA PHAM, JOHN KELLEY, JENNIFER MCHUGH'S Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Tamera Carlin And Jonathan Fornaci Or In the Alt/Protective Order Against Further Discovery. PLAINTIFFS MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS OF TAMARA CARLIN AND JONATHAN FORNACI IS GRANTED. THESE DEPOSITIONS ARE LIMITED TO THE FOLLOWING AREAS: 1)THE PERSONAL AND PROFESSIONAL RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN FORNACI AND CARLIN; 2)THE LOANS EXTENDED BY CARLIN TO REMOLONA; 3)CARLIN'S ATTEMPTS TO FORECLOSE ON THE PROPERTY; 4)CARLIN'S FINANCIAL WHEREWITHALL TO EXTEND THE LOAN; 5)FORNACI'S ACTIIVITES WHEN HE OBTAINED AND SOUGHT TO OBTAIN LOANS FOR MS. REMOLONA THROUGHOUT 2008. THESE AREAS ARE RELEVANT AND DO NOT SEEK PRIVILEGED INFORMATION. EACH DEPOSITION IS LIMITED TO 4 HOURS EACH. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: CELINA PHAM et al VS. JANET REMOLONA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Continued Deposition Of John Kelley An Dcelina Pham, Per Ccp Section 2025.450, Subd(A); And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 28, 2011, line 1, DEFENDANTS JANET REMOLONA, LILLY BURKS, IMG UNIVERSAL, INC., JONATHAN FORNACI, TAMARA CARLIN'S Motion To Compel Continued Deposition Of John Kelley An Dcelina Pham, Per CCP Section 2025.450, Subd(A); And Request For Monetary Sanctions. DEFENDANTS MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF JOHN KELLEY AND CELINE PHAM IS GRANTED. THE DEPOSITIONS ARE LIMITED TO THE AREAS DEFINED IN THE PREVIOUSLY SERVICED NOTICES. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK HELLAND VS. RAY BRONNER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration; Defts Affidavit
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 28, 2011, line 1, DEFENDANT RAY BRONNER'S Motion For Reconsideration. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE JANUARY 20 ORDER GRANTING A TERMINATION SANCTION IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CCP 1008(a) IN THAT NO NEW FACTS OR LAW HAVE BEEN PRESENTED, NOR A DECLARATION AS TO WHAT NEW FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCE OR LAW ARE CLAIMED TO BE SHOWN. FURTHER, DEFENDANT DOES NOT PROVIDE A REASONABLE EXCUSE AS TO WHY HE COULD NOT HAVE OPPOSED THE MOTION TO TERMINATE. =(302/LMG)
